mission.name: Lost Signal
objectives: Trace the dormant Apollo-band signal source.
Keep the rover crew alive until a stable comms link is restored.
Recover enough anomaly data to justify a return window.
mission.briefing: Artemis-07 diverted after receiving a structured radio pulse from Shackleton Crater. Mission control lost clean contact twelve minutes later.

environment.anomaly: Apollo-band signal with repeating geometric carrier modulation
environment.visibility: Low-angle glare on the rim, deep shadow inside the crater

crew.name: Commander Alma Vasquez
crew.status: Coordinating the crater hold and crew assignments
crew.notes: Prioritizes crew survival over mission prestige. Keeping anxiety contained after the signal spike. Trait: Calm under cascading pressure. Flaw: Carries too much alone before delegating. Stake: Will not lose another mission under their command. Call sign: Aegis.

character.flaw: Carries too much alone before delegating
character.personalStake: Will not lose another mission under their command

crew.name: Chief Engineer Tunde Okafor

extra.label: O2 Sys
extra.detail: Life support stabilization and field repair

crew.status: Monitoring scrubber leaks and power routing
crew.notes: Knows the patched scrubber can fail if dust clogs the bypass a second time. Trait: Turns panic into procedure. Flaw: Pushes damaged systems long past the safe window. Stake: Knows the rover only gets one real failure before people start dying. Call sign: Patchbay.

character.specialty: Life support stabilization and field repair
character.flaw: Pushes damaged systems long past the safe window
character.personalStake: Knows the rover only gets one real failure before people start dying

crew.name: Dr. Imani Reyes
crew.status: Filtering anomaly harmonics from terrain reflections
crew.notes: Convinced the source is artificial and older than the current mission profile suggests. Trait: Curiosity sharp enough to feel dangerous. Flaw: Can chase understanding past the point of safety. Stake: Believes this signal could redefine humanity's place on the moon. Call sign: Spectra.

character.trait: Curiosity sharp enough to feel dangerous
character.flaw: Can chase understanding past the point of safety
character.personalStake: Believes this signal could redefine humanity's place on the moon

crew.name: Lt. Hana Park
crew.status: Prepping for a risky surface check near the rim beacon
crew.notes: Most capable EVA operator on site, but the suit breach margin is uncomfortably thin. Trait: Most comfortable at the edge of the risk envelope. Flaw: Understates injuries if the mission still has momentum. Stake: Needs this mission to prove the last surface accident was not a career-ending failure. Call sign: Waypoint.

character.trait: Most comfortable at the edge of the risk envelope
character.flaw: Understates injuries if the mission still has momentum
character.personalStake: Needs this mission to prove the last surface accident was not a career-ending failure

eventLog.msg: Anomaly signal detected from Shackleton Crater interior.
Telemetry confirms the signal is repeating in deliberate geometric bursts.
Long-range comms degraded after the rover crossed the rim shadow line.
Okafor patched the primary scrubber bypass after a dust-line leak.
Artemis-07 rover reached the crater rim with all crew nominal.

narration:
The rover eases to a halt on the knife-edge rim of Shackleton Crater, and every speaker in the cabin hisses with an impossible transmission. The signal is old Apollo-band hardware, dead for decades, yet it is pulsing from the darkness below with machine-perfect rhythm.

Dr. Imani Reyes has already stripped the noise away once and the pattern only became stranger. Chief Engineer Tunde Okafor warns the scrubber patch may not survive a long delay. Lt. Hana Park is halfway into a damaged EVA suit. Commander Alma Vasquez, the crew is looking to you.
